artifacts.error:
Anthropic returned invalid plan JSON after retry: Response is not valid JSON.. Raw response: ```json
{
  "workout_plan": {
    "overview": "Hey Divya,

Great to have you on board! I know night shifts at the hospital make consistency tough, but three solid gym sessions a week is exactly where we need to start for fat loss. We're going to build around squats and rows since you enjoy them, and keep things simple and effective while your body adapts. No burpees in sight, promise.

## Warmup Routine (Do this before every training day)

Spend about 10 minutes getting your body ready. Start with 5 minutes of brisk walking on the treadmill or light cycling to get your heart rate up and blood flowing. Then move through some joint mobility — arm circles forward and back, leg swings front-to-back and side-to-side, hip circles, and some bodyweight squats. Finish with a few cat-cows for your spine and some standing torso rotations. Before you get into your main lifts, run through a couple of lighter warm-up sets — maybe just the bar or very light dumbbells — to groove the movement pattern.

## Day 1 — Lower Body Power

Aim for 8,000-9,000 steps today.

**Barbell Back Squat** — 4 sets x 5 reps
This is your favourite, so let's lead with it. Keep your core braced tight, chest up, and control the descent. Rest 3 minutes between sets.

**Romanian Deadlift (Barbell)** — 3 sets x 6 reps
Focus on the hip hinge here — push your hips back, feel the stretch in your hamstrings, and drive through your heels coming up. These will help your posterior chain strength and are fantastic for fat loss.

**Leg Press** — 3 sets x 12 reps
Feet shoulder-width, push through your heels. Don't lock out completely at the top — keep tension on the quads.

**Walking Lunges (Dumbbell)** — 3 sets x 10 reps per leg
These will challenge your balance since you're a beginner, so start light. Keep your torso upright and step far enough forward that your front knee stays behind your toes.

**Seated Leg Curl** — 3 sets x 15 reps
Squeeze at the top of each rep, control the negative.

**Cable Crunches** — 3 sets x 15 reps
Think about pulling your ribcage to your pelvis, not just yanking with your arms.

**Plank Hold** — 3 sets x 30-45 seconds
Keep your hips level, don't let them sag or pike up.

**Russian Twists (Bodyweight or light dumbbell)** — 3 sets x 20 total reps
Slow and controlled, rotate from your torso, not just your arms.

## Day 2 — Active Recovery

Aim for 6,000-7,000 steps today.

No gym today. Go for a 20-30 minute walk at an easy pace — this helps with recovery and keeps your daily energy expenditure up without taxing your system. Spend 10-15 minutes doing some gentle stretching or yoga flows. Focus on your hips, hamstrings, quads, and lower back since we hit legs yesterday. Child's pose, pigeon stretch, and some cat-cows will feel great.

## Day 3 — Upper Body Push & Pull

Aim for 8,000-9,000 steps today.

**Barbell Bench Press** — 4 sets x 5 reps
Plant your feet, squeeze your shoulder blades together, and press with control. This is a strength builder, so rest 3 minutes between sets.

**Bent-Over Barbell Row** — 4 sets x 6 reps
Your other favourite! Hinge at the hips, keep your back flat, and pull the bar to your lower chest. Think about driving your elbows back, not just yanking with your arms. Rest 2-3 minutes.

**Dumbbell Shoulder Press (Seated)** — 3 sets x 10 reps
Press straight up, don't arch your lower back excessively. Exhale as you press.

**Lat Pulldown (Cable)** — 3 sets x 12 reps
Pull to your upper chest, lean back slightly, and squeeze your shoulder blades together at the bottom.

**Cable Chest Fly** — 3 sets x 15 reps
Slight bend in the elbows, focus on squeezing your chest at the midpoint.

**Dumbbell Bicep Curl** — 3 sets x 12 reps
No swinging — keep your elbows pinned at your sides.

**Overhead Cable Tricep Extension** — 3 sets x 15 reps
Keep your elbows still, only your forearms should move.

**Hanging Knee Raises (or Lying Leg Raises if hanging is too tough)** — 3 sets x 12 reps
Raise with control, don't swing.

**Bicycle Crunches** — 3 sets x 20 total reps
Slow tempo, focus on the rotation.

**Side Plank** — 3 sets x 20-30 seconds per side
Stack your feet, keep your hips up.

## Day 4 — Rest Day

Aim for 5,000-6,000 steps today.

Full rest. Your body needs this to recover and adapt. Light walking is fine if you feel like moving, but no structured training. Focus on sleep, hydration, and eating well.

## Day 5 — Lower Body Hypertrophy

Aim for 8,000-9,000 steps today.

**Goblet Squat (Dumbbell)** — 4 sets x 12 reps
Hold a dumbbell at chest height, squat deep, and really focus on feeling your quads and glutes work. Rest 90 seconds between sets.

**Bulgarian Split Squat (Dumbbell)** — 3 sets x 10 reps per leg
These are tough but amazing for building your legs and improving balance. Rear foot elevated on a bench, front foot far enough forward that your knee doesn't travel past your toes.

**Leg Extension** — 3 sets x 15 reps
Squeeze hard at the top, control the descent.

**Lying Leg Curl** — 3 sets x 15 reps
Focus on the contraction, don't just swing the weight.

**Cable Hip Abduction** — 3 sets x 20 reps per leg
Great for glute medius, which helps with hip stability.

**Reverse Crunches** — 3 sets x 15 reps
Lift your hips off the ground, don't just swing your legs.

**Dead Bug** — 3 sets x 12 reps per side
Press your lower back into the floor, move slowly and with control.

**Mountain Climbers (slow tempo)** — 3 sets x 20 total reps
Since you're avoiding burpees, these give you a bit of dynamic core work without the jump.

## Day 6 — Upper Body Volume

Aim for 8,000-9,000 steps today.

**Dumbbell Bench Press** — 4 sets x 10 reps
Similar to barbell bench but allows a bit more range of motion. Control the dumbbells down, press up with power.

**Seated Cable Row** — 4 sets x 12 reps
Pull to your lower chest, squeeze your shoulder blades together, control the release.

**Dumbbell Lateral Raise** — 3 sets x 15 reps
Lift to shoulder height, slight bend in the elbows, control the descent.

**Cable Face Pull** — 3 sets x 20 reps
Pull to your face, elbows high, great for rear delts and upper back health.

**Incline Dumbbell Curl** — 3 sets x 12 reps
Let your arms hang fully, curl with control, squeeze at the top.

**Cable Tricep Pushdown** — 3 sets x 15 reps
Elbows pinned, full extension at the bottom.

**Cable Woodchop** — 3 sets x 12 reps per side
Rotational core work — pull diagonally across your body, engage your obliques.

**Plank to Downward Dog** — 3 sets x 10 reps
Start in plank, push your hips up into downward dog, return to plank. Great for core and shoulder stability.

**Hollow Body Hold** — 3 sets x 20-30 seconds
Press your lower back into the floor, arms overhead, legs extended and hovering.

## Day 7 — Rest Day

Aim for 5,000-6,000 steps today.

Another full rest day. Use this time to meal prep for the week ahead if you can, and prioritize getting as much sleep as your schedule allows.

## Training Intensity

For the big compound lifts — squats, deadlifts, bench press, rows — leave one or two reps in the tank. You should finish each set knowing you could have done one more rep with good form, but you stop there. This keeps you safe and ensures you're training hard without burning out. For the isolation and accessory work — leg curls, bicep curls, lateral raises — you can push closer to failure. On the last set of these exercises, if you hit failure with strict form, you can do one or two controlled "cheat reps" where you use a tiny bit of momentum to keep going, but only after you've hit true failure with perfect form. Never do cheat reps on squats, deadlifts, RDLs, lunges, or any barbell compound movement — those always stay strict.

## Cooldown & Stretching

After each training session, spend 5-10 minutes stretching the muscles you just worked. Hold each stretch for 30-60 seconds per side, breathe deeply, and don't bounce. For lower body days, hit your quads (standing quad stretch), hamstrings (seated forward fold or standing toe touch), glutes (figure-four stretch), hip flexors (low lunge), and calves (calf stretch against the wall). For upper body days, stretch your chest (doorway pec stretch), lats (overhead side bend or hanging from the bar), shoulders (cross-body shoulder stretch), triceps (overhead tricep stretch), and biceps (doorway bicep stretch with arm extended). This will help with recovery and flexibility over time.

## Sleep Strategy

I know night shifts make sleep brutal, but we need to aim for 7.5-9 hours whenever possible. With your schedule, try to sleep right after your morning workout and breakfast — block out light with blackout curtains or an eye mask, and keep your room cool. Set your phone to Do Not Disturb so you're not woken up by notifications. Here are some practical tips that'll help:

- Cut off chai and coffee at least 6-8 hours before you plan to sleep. I know chai is life, but the caffeine will mess with your sleep quality even if you feel tired.
- Avoid heavy meals within 2-3 hours of sleep. Since you eat dinner at 8 pm before your shift, try to keep your post-shift breakfast lighter if you're going straight to bed — maybe something like curd rice and a banana instead of a heavy paratha.
- Put your phone and screens away at least 30-60 minutes before bed. The blue light tricks your brain into staying awake.
- Try some deep breathing or a short meditation before bed — even 5 minutes of slow breathing can help signal to your body that it's time to wind down.
- Keep your room as dark and quiet as possible. If noise is an issue, earplugs or a white noise app can help.
- Try to keep your sleep schedule as consistent as possible even on off days — your body thrives on routine.
- A warm shower before bed can help lower your core temperature afterward, which signals sleep time.

## Your Week Ahead

Divya, this is a solid starting point. Three training days gives your body time to recover, and we're hitting everything you need for fat loss — strength work to preserve muscle, higher-rep accessory work to build volume, and enough movement throughout the week to keep your metabolism humming. Your step targets are realistic given your nursing schedule, and I've kept the sessions right around 60 minutes so they fit your morning post-shift window. Focus on learning the movements this week, don't worry about lifting heavy yet. Consistency is your superpower here — just show up, do the work, and trust the process. You've got this.",
    "days": []
  },
  "nutrition_plan": {
    "calories": 0,
    "protein": 0,
    "carbs": 0,
    "fat": 0,
    "meals": []
  },
  "cardio_plan": {
    "sessions": []
  },
  "supplement_plan": {
    "items": []
  },
  "coach_notes": ""
}
```
